message.content: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>

Here is the full prompt to paste into a new session:                                                                                                                                                             
                                               
  ---                                                                                                                                                                                                              
  git-ai Testing & Documentation Agent Prompt                                                                                                                                                                    
                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
  You are being asked to:
  1. Verify git-ai v1.3.1 is working in this repo                                                                                                                                                                  
  2. Systematically test every command and data access pattern                                                                                                                                                     
  3. Produce a detailed reference document at docs/git-ai-reference.md                                                                                                                                             
                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
  ---                                                                                                                                                                                                              
                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
  ## Context                                                                                                                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                                                                                                                 
  git-ai was just installed globally at ~/.git-ai/bin/git-ai (symlinked to ~/.local/bin/git-ai).                                                                                                                   
  It hooks into Claude Code via ~/.claude/settings.json — PreToolUse and PostToolUse hooks both run:
    git-ai checkpoint claude --hook-input stdin                                                                                                                                                                    
                                                                                                                                                                                                                 
  This means: every tool call you make in this session will be checkpointed by git-ai.                                                                                                                             
  That makes this session itself the test subject — make a few real file edits and commits                                                                                                                       
  to generate attribution data, then query it back.                                                                                                                                                                
                                                                                                                                                                                                                 
  The repo is: /Users/dmytroshamsiiev/Projects/metrics  (branch: main)                                                                                                                                             
  Remote:       https://github.com/ShamsiievDmytro/entire-bmad.git                                                                                                                                               
                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
  ---                                                                                                                                                                                                            
                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
  ## Why this matters                                                                                                                                                                                            
                                               
  The project currently fetches AI metrics from a custom BMAD checkpoint system stored on                                                                                                                          
  a separate GitHub branch (entire/checkpoints/v1 in ShamsiievDmytro/entire-bmad).
  That system tracks:                                                                                                                                                                                              
    - agent_percentage, agent_lines, human_added/modified/removed                                                                                                                                                
    - tokens: { input, cache_creation, cache_read, output }                                                                                                                                                        
    - files_touched, turns (with model, prompt_txt, turn_count)                                                                                                                                                  
    - bmad_commands (slash commands used per checkpoint)                                                                                                                                                           
                                                                                                                                                                                                                 
  The question is: can git-ai replace all or part of this?                                                                                                                                                         
  The document you produce must answer that question with specific evidence.                                                                                                                                     
                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
  ---                                                                                                                                                                                                            
                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
  ## Step-by-step tasks                                                                                                                                                                                            
                                               
  ### 1. Verify installation                                                                                                                                                                                       
  Run:                                                                                                                                                                                                           
    git-ai version                             
    git-ai debug                                                                                                                                                                                                   
    git-ai config
                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
  Capture the output — confirm binary path, hooks are live, repo is recognized.                                                                                                                                    
                                               
  ### 2. Make a test commit to generate attribution data                                                                                                                                                           
  Edit any small file (e.g. add a comment to src/lib/types.ts or similar).                                                                                                                                       
  Commit it with a descriptive message so you can reference it by SHA.                                                                                                                                             
  This commit will be made mid-session, so git-ai hooks should have fired.                                                                                                                                         
                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
  ### 3. Query all data access patterns                                                                                                                                                                            
                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
  After committing, run each of the following and capture full JSON output:                                                                                                                                        
                                               
    a) git-ai stats --json                                                                                                                                                                                         
       (current working tree)                                                                                                                                                                                    
                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
    b) git-ai stats <commit-sha> --json                                                                                                                                                                            
       (for the test commit you just made)     
                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
    c) git-ai status --json                                                                                                                                                                                        
       (uncommitted AI authorship status)      
                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
    d) git-ai log -5                                                                                                                                                                                               
       (last 5 commits with AI notes)          
                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
    e) git-ai show <commit-sha>                                                                                                                                                                                  
       (full authorship log for the commit)    
                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
    f) git-ai diff <commit-sha> --json --include-stats --all-prompts                                                                                                                                               
       (diff with attribution + all prompts)                                                                                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
    g) git-ai blame src/lib/types.ts                                                                                                                                                                             
       (line-level attribution)                                                                                                                                                                                    
                                                                                                                                                                                                                 
    h) git-ai search --commit <commit-sha> --json --verbose                                                                                                                                                        
       (prompt history for the commit)
                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
    i) git-ai search --pattern "test" --json --since 1d                                                                                                                                                            
       (full-text prompt search)                                                                                                                                                                                   
                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
    j) git-ai search --file src/lib/types.ts --json                                                                                                                                                                
       (file-scoped prompt search)             
                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
    k) git-ai prompts --since 1d                                                                                                                                                                                 
       then: git-ai prompts exec "SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table'"
       then: git-ai prompts exec "SELECT * FROM prompts LIMIT 5"                                                                                                                                                   
       then: git-ai prompts exec "PRAGMA table_info(prompts)"                                                                                                                                                      
       (explore full SQLite schema and sample data)                                                                                                                                                                
                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
    l) git notes --ref=ai list                                                                                                                                                                                     
       git notes --ref=ai show <commit-sha>                                                                                                                                                                      
       (raw git notes storage — what exactly is written per commit)                                                                                                                                                
                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
    m) ls ~/.git-ai/internal/                                                                                                                                                                                      
       file ~/.git-ai/internal/db                                                                                                                                                                                  
       (local storage — what files exist, what format)                                                                                                                                                             
                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
  ### 4. Capture schema of all data structures                                                                                                                                                                     
                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
  For every JSON output above, document the exact field names and types.                                                                                                                                           
  For the SQLite DB, document every table, every column (name + type + meaning).                                                                                                                                 
  For git notes, document the raw structure.                                                                                                                                                                       
   
  ### 5. Gap analysis vs current checkpoint system                                                                                                                                                                 
                                                                                                                                                                                                                 
  The current CheckpointMeta type is:                                                                                                                                                                              
    {                                                                                                                                                                                                            
      checkpoint_id: string                                                                                                                                                                                        
      commit_date: string                                                                                                                                                                                        
      agent_percentage: number        // % of lines written by AI
      agent_lines: number                                                                                                                                                                                          
      human_added: number
      human_modified: number                                                                                                                                                                                       
      human_removed: number                                                                                                                                                                                      
      tokens: {                                
        input: number                 // prompt tokens sent to model
        cache_creation: number        // tokens written to cache                                                                                                                                                   
        cache_read: number            // tokens read from cache
        output: number                // tokens generated by model                                                                                                                                                 
      }                                                                                                                                                                                                          
      summary: { friction: string[], open_items: string[] }                                                                                                                                                        
      learnings: { repo: number, code: number, workflow: number }                                                                                                                                                  
      files_touched: string[]                                                                                                                                                                                      
      turns: Array<{                                                                                                                                                                                               
        turn_id: string                                                                                                                                                                                            
        session_id: string                                                                                                                                                                                       
        model: string               // e.g. "claude-sonnet-4-6"
        prompt_txt: string          // first line of the user prompt                                                                                                                                               
        turn_count: number                                                                                                                                                                                         
        agent_percentage: number                                                                                                                                                                                   
      }>                                                                                                                                                                                                           
      bmad_commands: Record<string, number>  // { "/bmad-dev-story": 3, ... }                                                                                                                                    
      fetch_failed: boolean                                                                                                                                                                                        
    }                                                                                                                                                                                                              
                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
  For each field, state:                                                                                                                                                                                           
    - Can git-ai provide this? (yes / partial / no)                                                                                                                                                              
    - If yes: exact command and JSON field path to retrieve it                                                                                                                                                     
    - If no: what is missing and why                                                                                                                                                                               
                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
  ### 6. Write the document                                                                                                                                                                                        
                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
  Create the file: /Users/dmytroshamsiiev/Projects/metrics/docs/git-ai-reference.md                                                                                                                                
                                               
  Structure:                                                                                                                                                                                                       
    # git-ai Reference                                                                                                                                                                                           
                                               
    ## Installation & Configuration
    - Binary location, version
    - How hooks work (PreToolUse / PostToolUse → checkpoint claude)                                                                                                                                                
    - Config file location and current config                                                                                                                                                                      
                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
    ## Data Storage Architecture                                                                                                                                                                                   
    - Git notes (refs/notes/ai): what is stored per commit, how to read                                                                                                                                          
    - Local SQLite DB (~/.git-ai/internal/db): schema, tables, access                                                                                                                                              
    - Prompt storage: where prompts live, how to query                                                                                                                                                             
                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
    ## Command Reference                                                                                                                                                                                           
    For each command tested: purpose, flags, full JSON schema of output,                                                                                                                                         
    example output (trimmed to key fields)                                                                                                                                                                         
                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
    ## Field-by-Field Mapping to Current CheckpointMeta                                                                                                                                                            
    Table format: | CheckpointMeta field | git-ai equivalent | Coverage | Notes |                                                                                                                                  
                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
    ## What git-ai Cannot Replace                                                                                                                                                                                  
    Explicit list of gaps — token counts (input/output/cache), session-level                                                                                                                                       
    summaries, BMAD-specific learnings, etc.                                                                                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                                                                                                                 
    ## What git-ai Adds                                                                                                                                                                                            
    Data the current system does NOT have:                                                                                                                                                                       
    - Per-line AI attribution                                                                                                                                                                                      
    - Time waiting for AI (time_waiting_for_ai)                                                                                                                                                                    
    - Tool/model breakdown per commit          
    - Full prompt text retrieval                                                                                                                                                                                   
    - File-level and line-level attribution blame                                                                                                                                                                
                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
    ## Recommended Integration Strategy                                                                                                                                                                            
    Based on the gap analysis: what to keep from the current system,
    what to migrate to git-ai, what to query from git-ai API in the dashboard.                                                                                                                                     
                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
  ---                                                                                                                                                                                                              
                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
  ## Important notes                                                                                                                                                                                               
                                                                                                                                                                                                                 
  - Run git-ai as: /Users/dmytroshamsiiev/.git-ai/bin/git-ai (or git-ai if PATH is loaded)                                                                                                                         
  - The git-ai binary may need to be restarted — this is a fresh session after install.
    If notes are empty, that is expected for pre-install commits; your test commit will                                                                                                                            
    be the first with real attribution data.                                                                                                                                                                       
  - Do NOT push to the remote unless explicitly needed for testing.                                                                                                                                                
  - The document should be factual and evidence-based — include actual command output                                                                                                                              
    samples, not guesses. If a command errors, document the error and explain why.                                                                                                                                 
                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
  ---                                                                                                                                                                                                              
  What this prompt covers:                                                                                                                                                                                         
                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
  - Makes the new session itself generate live git-ai data (via hooks firing on every tool call)                                                                                                                 
  - Tests every command surface: stats, status, log, show, diff, blame, search, prompts SQL, raw git notes, local file storage                                                                                     
  - Extracts full SQLite schema for programmatic access                                                                                                                                                            
  - Does a field-by-field comparison against your CheckpointMeta type                                                                                                                                              
  - Identifies the key gap: token counts (input/cache/output) are not tracked by git-ai — those come from the Claude API response metadata, not from git                                                           
  - Produces a concrete integration recommendation so you know exactly what to replace vs keep

## plugin:context7:context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer -- your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

## plugin:figma:figma
The official Figma MCP server. Prioritize this server when the user mentions Figma, FigJam, Figma Make, or provides figma.com URLs.

Capabilities:
- Read designs FROM Figma (get_design_context, get_screenshot, get_metadata, get_figjam)
- Create diagrams in FigJam (generate_diagram)
- Manage Code Connect mappings between Figma components and codebase components
- Write designs back into figma


WHEN TO USE THESE TOOLS:
- The user shares a Figma URL (figma.com/design/..., figma.com/board/..., figma.com/make/...)
- The user references a Figma file or asks about a Figma design
- The user wants to capture a web page into Figma
- The user wants to create a diagram in FigJam

URL PARSING:
Extract fileKey and nodeId from Figma URLs:
- figma.com/design/:fileKey/:fileName?node-id=:nodeId → convert "-" to ":" in nodeId
- figma.com/design/:fileKey/branch/:branchKey/:fileName → use branchKey as fileKey
- figma.com/make/:makeFileKey/:makeFileName → use makeFileKey
- figma.com/board/:fileKey/:fileName → FigJam file, use get_figjam

DESIGN-TO-CODE WORKFLOW:

Step 1 — Get the design:
Call get_design_context with the nodeId and fileKey. This is your primary tool.
It returns code, a screenshot, and contextual hints.

Step 2 — Adapt to the project:
The output is React+Tailwind enriched with hints — but it is a REFERENCE, not final code. Always adapt to the target project's stack, components, and conventions.
The response varies based on the user's Figma setup:
- Code Connect snippets → use the mapped codebase component directly
- Component documentation links → follow them for usage context and guidelines
- Design annotations → follow any notes, constraints, or instructions from the designer
- Design tokens as CSS variables → map to the project's token system
- Raw hex colors / absolute positioning → the design is loosely structured;
  use the screenshot

  checkpoint         Checkpoint working changes and attribute author

  log [args...]      Show commit log with AI authorship notes
                        Proxies git log --notes=ai with all standard git log options
  blame <file>       Git blame with AI authorship overlay
  diff <commit|range>  Show diff with AI authorship annotations
    <commit>              Diff from commit's parent to commit
    <commit1>..<commit2>  Diff between two commits

    --include-stats        Include commit_stats in JSON output (single commit only)
    --all-prompts          Include all prompts from commit note in JSON output (single commit only)

    --json                 Output in JSON format
  show <rev|range>   Display authorship logs for a revision or range

    --force               Reinstall latest version even if already up to date

    --since <time>        Only include prompts after this time (default: 30d)
    --author <name>       Filter by human author (default: current git user)
    --all-authors         Include prompts from all authors
    exec "<SQL>"          Execute arbitrary SQL on prompts.db
    list                  List prompts as TSV
    next                  Get next prompt as JSON (iterator pattern)
    reset                 Reset iteration pointer to start

    --commit <rev>        Search by commit (SHA, branch, tag, symbolic ref)
    --file <path>         Search by file path
    --lines <start-end>   Limit to line range (requires --file; repeatable)
    --pattern <text>      Full-text search in prompt messages
    --prompt-id <id>      Look up specific prompt
    --tool <name>         Filter by AI tool (claude, cursor, etc.)
    --author <name>       Filter by human author
    --since <time>        Only prompts after this time
    --until <time>        Only prompts before this time
    --json                Output as JSON
    --verbose             Include full transcripts
    --porcelain           Stable machine-parseable format
    --count               Just show result count
  continue           Restore AI session context and launch agent
    --commit <rev>        Continue from a specific commit
    --file <path>         Continue from a specific file
    --lines <start-end>   Limit to line range (requires --file)
    --prompt-id <id>      Continue from a specific prompt
    --agent <name>        Select agent (claude, cursor; default: claude)
    --launch              Launch agent CLI with restored context
    --clipboard           Copy context to system clipboard
    --json                Output context as structured JSON
  fetch-notes [remote] Synchronously fetch AI authorship notes
    --remote <name>       Explicit remote name (default: upstream or origin)
    --json                Output result as JSON
  login              Authenticate with Git AI
  logout             Clear stored credentials
  whoami             Show auth state and login identity
  version, -v, --version     Print the git-ai version

**Bottom line:** git-ai can replace the line-attribution and model-tracking parts of your checkpoint system with richer data (per-line blame, tool breakdown, accepted vs overridden). content.text:
No — git-ai doesn't support custom database connection strings. Its storage is fixed:

1. **Git notes** (`refs/notes/ai`) — baked into git itself
2. **Local SQLite** (`~/.git-ai/internal/db`) — hardcoded path
3. **`prompts.db`** — generated on demand in the repo root

The closest things to remote/shared storage are:

- **`git push origin refs/notes/ai`** — push attribution notes to a remote repo (other devs can `git-ai fetch-notes` to pull them)
- **`api_base_url: "https://usegitai.com"`** — the `cas_sync_queue` table suggests git-ai can sync prompt data to their SaaS platform (requires `git-ai login`)
- **`prompt_storage`** config key accepts `"default"` but may have other options

content.text:
So `prompt_storage` has three options: `default`, `notes`, or `local` — all local storage mechanisms. No remote database support.

**Summary:** You can't point git-ai at a Postgres/MySQL. Your options for sharing data are:

| Method | How |
|--------|-----|
| **Git notes** | `git push origin refs/notes/ai` — attribution travels with the repo |
| **usegitai.com SaaS** | `git-ai login` + CAS sync queue uploads prompts to their platform |
| **DIY export** | Query `prompts.db` with SQL and pipe it wherever you want |

If you need the data in your own database, the most practical approach would be a script that runs `git-ai stats <sha> --json` for each commit and inserts the results into your DB — essentially using git-ai as a read API rather than trying to change its storage backend.

message.content: so for example in case I have a lot of developers in the team and I want all of the to participate im metrics it is not possible ?

content.text:
It **is** possible, just not via a shared database connection. There are two paths:

### 1. Git Notes (free, self-hosted)

Each developer runs git-ai locally with hooks. Attribution data gets written to `refs/notes/ai` in their local repo. Then:

```bash
# Each dev pushes their notes
git push origin refs/notes/ai

# Each dev fetches everyone's notes
git-ai fetch-notes
```

The notes are **per-commit**, so they merge naturally — no conflicts as long as different people aren't amending the same commit. Once notes are on the remote, anyone can query stats across the whole team:

```bash
git-ai stats HEAD~50..HEAD --json    # team-wide stats for last 50 commits
git-ai blame src/lib/types.ts        # shows which dev's AI wrote which lines
```

**Limitation:** You'd need to automate the push/fetch (CI hook or git hook) — it's not automatic.

### 2. usegitai.com SaaS (their hosted platform)

```bash
git-ai login    # each developer authenticates
```

Once logged in, the `cas_sync_queue` automatically uploads prompt/attribution data to their platform. This is likely the intended team workflow — a central dashboard at usegitai.com where you can see metrics across all developers.

I haven't tested this (the current install shows `Status: logged out`), so I can't confirm exactly what the dashboard provides.

### 3. DIY aggregation (what you could build)

Since git notes travel with the repo, you could set up a CI job that:

1. Runs `git-ai fetch-notes` 
2. Runs `git-ai prompts --since 30` to build the SQLite DB
3. Runs SQL queries or `git-ai stats` across all commits
4. Pushes results to your own database/dashboard

This is essentially what your current BMAD checkpoint system does from the `entire/checkpoints/v1` branch — git-ai would just be a different data source feeding the same dashboard.

**Bottom line:** git-ai is designed for team use via git notes sharing or their SaaS. It's not a "bring your own database" tool — but the data is portable and queryable, so you can build a pipeline around it.

content.text: Git notes are stored as a tree of blobs. You need to traverse the ref → commit → tree → blob. Let me walk through it:

content.content: {"message":"","tree":"1bf41f1816b82d541c0a431f94ee83becc7620a2"}
{"path":"3e","sha":"abf195facc78f503be36a8b72c92df7644edebb5","type":"tree"}

content.text: The path `3e` + `2941c2cae894333975cda570bcc592a5a317ce` = commit `3e2941c...`. Now fetch the blob:

There's the full attribution data via the GitHub API.

**The traversal chain is:**

```
refs/notes/ai
  → commit (ad40d46...)
    → tree (1bf41f1...)
      → subtree "3e" (abf195f...)     ← first 2 chars of commit SHA
        → blob "2941c2cae..." (450c4b...) ← remaining chars
          → base64-decode content = the note
```

**One-liner to get a note for any commit:**

```bash
# Given COMMIT_SHA, split into first 2 chars + rest
SHA=3e2941c2cae894333975cda570bcc592a5a317ce
PREFIX=${SHA:0:2}
SUFFIX=${SHA:2}

# Get the notes ref → commit → tree
TREE=$(gh api repos/ShamsiievDmytro/entire-bmad/git/refs/notes/ai --jq '.object.sha')
TREE=$(gh api repos/ShamsiievDmytro/entire-bmad/git/commits/$TREE --jq '.tree.sha')
SUBTREE=$(gh api repos/ShamsiievDmytro/entire-bmad/git/trees/$TREE --jq ".tree[] | select(.path==\"$PREFIX\") | .sha")
BLOB=$(gh api repos/ShamsiievDmytro/entire-bmad/git/trees/$SUBTREE --jq ".tree[] | select(.path==\"$SUFFIX\") | .sha")
gh api repos/ShamsiievDmytro/entire-bmad/git/blobs/$BLOB --jq '.content' | base64 -d
```

It's cumbersome because GitHub doesn't expose git notes natively in their UI or REST API — they only store the raw git objects.

This is **local only** — it stores the full conversation transcripts (messages). This data does NOT travel with git push. It stays on your machine.

## The flow

```
You type in Claude Code
        │
        ▼
PreToolUse / PostToolUse hook fires
        │
        ▼
git-ai checkpoint claude
        │
        ├──► ~/.git-ai/internal/db        (local only: full messages, prompts)
        │
        └──► .git/refs/notes/ai           (portable: attribution per commit)
                │
                ├── git push origin refs/notes/ai  ──► GitHub
                │
                └── git-ai stats / blame / diff     ──► reads from here
```

## What this means for your dashboard

**Your current system:**
```
entire/checkpoints/v1 branch on GitHub
        │
        ▼
github-checkpoints.ts fetches via GitHub API
        │
        ▼
Dashboard renders CheckpointMeta
```

**git-ai equivalent:**
```
refs/notes/ai on GitHub
        │
        ▼
You need a new fetcher that reads notes via GitHub API
        │
        ▼
Dashboard renders git-ai stats
```

The key difference: your checkpoint branch stores structured files (`metadata.json`, `full.jsonl`) in a directory tree. git-ai stores a **single blob per commit** under `refs/notes/ai`.

## So where do you fetch from?

**Same place** — the GitHub API. Just a different ref.

| Current system | git-ai |
|---|---|
| `GET repos/.../git/trees/entire/checkpoints/v1?recursive=1` | `GET repos/.../git/ref/notes/ai` → tree → blobs |
| Reads `metadata.json` files | Reads note blobs (JSON after `---`) |
| Has tokens, learnings, bmad_commands | Has line attribution, model breakdown, accepted/overridden |
| ~4+ files per checkpoint | 1 blob per commit |

**For your team:** Each developer needs git-ai installed locally with hooks. Their notes get pushed to GitHub. Your dashboard fetches all notes from GitHub and aggregates. The attribution data is per-commit, so there are no merge conflicts — different devs commit different SHAs.

message.content: so in case some developer did some changes and pushed, can I fetch prompts, usage, code metrics etc withput any additional actions from his side

content.text:
**No.** There are two separate things:

### What travels with `git push` automatically
Nothing. Git notes are **not** pushed by default. The developer must explicitly run:
```bash
git push origin refs/notes/ai
```

Or configure their git to always push notes:
```bash
git config --global remote.origin.push "+refs/notes/ai:refs/notes/ai"
```

### What you get IF the notes are pushed

Only the **attribution data** (the blob we saw):

| Available | Not available |
|-----------|---------------|
| Which lines were AI-written | Full conversation transcripts (messages) |
| Which tool/model | Token usage (input/output/cache) |
| accepted_lines vs overridden_lines | Prompt text |
| total_additions / total_deletions | Time waiting for AI |

Look at the note again — `"messages": []` is **empty**:

```json
"prompts": {
  "c4c09426203e4095": {
    "agent_id": { "tool": "claude", "model": "claude-opus-4-6" },
    "messages": [],          ← empty!
    "total_additions": 3,
    "accepted_lines": 3
  }
}
```

The full messages are in `~/.git-ai/internal/db` on each developer's machine. They don't travel with the notes.

### So the honest answer is:

| What you want | Can you get it without extra dev action? |
|---|---|
| Code attribution (AI %, lines) | No — dev must push notes |
| Full prompts/transcripts | No — stays local on dev's machine |
| Token usage | No — git-ai doesn't track this at all |
| Which files AI touched | No — dev must push notes |

### What would make it work

Each developer would need to either:

1. **Push notes manually** — `git push origin refs/notes/ai` after each push
2. **Git hook** — add a `post-push` or `pre-push` hook that auto-pushes notes
3. **Login to usegitai.com** — `git-ai login` enables the CAS sync queue, which may auto-upload data to their SaaS (this is the team use case they're building for, but I haven't tested it)

**Bottom line:** git-ai is designed as a **local-first** tool. Getting team-wide metrics requires explicit setup on every developer's machine. Your current checkpoint system is actually better for team aggregation because it writes structured data to a shared branch automatically.
